Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 And For Sanctions Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Ccp 128.6
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Meredith for Jean Judnick; Tom LaLanne for Stephen Cassolato; Kathryn Schofield (by CourtCall) for Carole Cassolato; John Judnick.

Mr. Meredith stated that the case had settled a year ago, with payment to be made within 15 days. He said that Mr. LaLanne's client has refused to provide the deed and transfer as required by the settlement. Mr. LaLanne responded that he has stated his willingness to provide the deed to Mr. Meredith, but that to comply with promises he made to Debra Dolch to obtain her resignation as trustee, he was seeking a waiver of accounting by Ms. Dolch from Mr. Cassolato, and an agreement that he could pay Ms. Dolch $3000 and her attorney Mr. Der $3000 for accrued fees. He stated that Mr. Meredith's client refuses to sign the waiver of accounting or agree to the payment of fees to Ms. Dolch and Mr. Der. He contended that the settlement agreement did not require any accounting to be made, and that Ms. Dolch had resigned in accordance with the settlement agreement, so there should not be any grounds to object to her payment. Ms. Schofield stated that she wanted to clarify that her client, Carole Cassolato, had done nothing wrong, and that there was no basis for seeking sanctions against her. Mr. Judnick stated that he was concerned about the failure to provide an accounting, and specify in writing what the assets and expenditures are. Mr. LaLanne proposed a one week continuance, with the respective parties to return to Court and perform a simultaneous exchange of documents in satisfaction of the settlement agreement and to bring the matter to closure. The Court inquired whether the beneficiaries would execute a dismissal of the action if they were provided with the deed in the absence of an accounting. Mr. Meredith stated that he would have to discuss that with his client. Mr. Judnick stated that he might not be willing to dismiss under those circumstances. The Court took a brief recess, and then returned to the bench. He noted that he had carefully reviewed the settlement agreement, and that there was no provision under its terms for requiring an accounting as a condition of the settlement. He stated that he was prepared to order that the deeds be turned over in compliance with the settlement agreement by no later than June 24, 2016. He stated that he was reserving the issue of sanctions. He directed Mr. Meredith as prevailing party to prepare an order in conformity with his ruling.
At the conclusion of the proceeding, and outside the presence of Judge Cheng, Mr. LaLanne provided a notarized deed to Mr. Meredith, along with a Request for Dismissal.

Carol Cassolato And Jean Judnicks Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathryn Schofield for Carol Cassolato. Roger Meredith for Jean & John Judnick. Thomas LaLanne for Stephen Cassolato.

Ms. Schofield stated that she would like further ruling on the other claims (other than Elder Abuse), that the conversion and negligence claims were over the statute of limitations and should be dismissed, that the fraud claim is factually insufficient, that the court had previously ruled on it, that it was filed late and requested that it be dismissed and that the opposition to this motion was filed late. Mr. LaLanne stated that this motion was not filed timely, cited statute 438(e) providing argument regarding the interpretation of the statute, that the opposition was late due to issues with File & Serve and the Memorial Day holiday, that the statute of limitations for an elder abuse claim is 4 years, that they did not specifically address conversion or the negligence claim in their demurrer, that you cannot allege clear and convincing evidence in an elder abuse claim, that financial elder abuse does not have to be plead or proven and that Stephen never received papers or evidence until long after the death of his father. Ms. Schofield stated that the standards are the same for elder abuse no matter what the claim is and that timeliness was addressed in their brief. Mr. Meredith stated that Mr. LaLanne is arguing a demurrer however this is not a demurrer, that fraud is required to be plead with specificity and that there are no facts in this claim. Mr. LaLanne stated that a motion for judgment on the pleadings is based on the same principles as a demurrer. In light of the July 6, 2015 trial date the court requested proposed orders be submitted no later than this Friday and that the order will be issued early next week.

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Order
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Meredith for and with Jean Judnick. Kathryn Schofield for and with Carol Cassolato. Tom Lalanne for Stephen Cassolato (Trustee). Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Temporary Trustee).

The Court started with the Motion to vacate order. Mr. LaLanne explained that it was his understanding that the hearing had been continued to the January 26th and he was not awared of the January 5th hearing and he did not appear on January 5, 2015 and the Order Suspending Trustee should be vacated. Furthmore, he wanted to present arguments today on the merit with respect to the petition to remove Stephen Cassolato as trustee. Mr. Meredith argued that he and co-counsel were both in court on January 5th and that they received the same notice. Ms. Scholfield and Mr. Meredith agreed to argue on the merit. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to action or lack of action taken by the Trustee with regard to the accounting and the elder abuse action. The Court stated that it is appropriated for Debra Dolch to continue as Temporary Trustee and it will issue an amended order.

With regard to the Cross-Petition, Ms. Schofield stated that it can be decided as a matter of law and it should be denied. Mr. Meredith stated that the Cross-Petition is really a demurrer, that it does not meet the requirement under the Health and Safety Code and that nothing changed between the first and second pleadings. Mr. LaLanne argued that that is what the claim is based on. Ms. Schofield provided the case background. The Court took a recess. After the recess, the Court questioned whether any response was filed to the cross-petition. Mr. Meredith stated that an objection was filed in June 2014. The Court explained the process that filing an objection does not get rid of the petition. The Court stated that to simplify things and noted on the record: 1)After hearing the argument and the position of the parties, it does not change the appropriateness of the January 5, 2015 order and that the suspension is in effect. 2)The Accounting is denied without prejudice. 3)The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. and the Cross-Petition are referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on July 6, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. (order for the same signed in open court and copies were provided to all counsel). Surcharge issue is continued.
